mission.title: Install and Configure Docker Desktop Kubernetes
mission.description: Docker Desktop for Mac provides an integrated Docker experience on macOS, including a local Kubernetes cluster. It's ideal for developers looking to test and develop applications in a Kubernetes environment without the overhead of managing a full cluster. However, it may not be suitable for production workloads due to resource limitations and lack of advanced features.

steps.title: Download Docker Desktop
steps.description: This step installs Docker Desktop, which includes Kubernetes support. Run: open https://store.docker.com/editions/community/docker-ce-desktop-mac. This opens the Docker Desktop download page.

steps.title: Install Docker Desktop
steps.description: Run the downloaded installer to set up Docker Desktop on your Mac. Run these commands in order: open ~/Downloads/Docker.dmg, sudo cp -R /Volumes/Docker/Docker.app /Applications/, and hdiutil unmount /Volumes/Docker. Replace 'Docker.dmg' with the actual filename if different. This copies Docker Desktop to your Applications folder and unmounts the Docker disk image.

steps.title: Start Docker Desktop and Enable Kubernetes
steps.description: Launch Docker Desktop and enable the Kubernetes feature in settings. Run these commands in order: open -a Docker, then wait for Docker to fully start, then open -a 'Docker' --args --enable-kubernetes. This starts Docker Desktop, waits for it to initialize, and enables Kubernetes.

steps.title: Verify Kubernetes Installation
steps.description: Check if Kubernetes is running correctly on Docker Desktop. Run: kubectl cluster-info and kubectl get nodes. These commands display cluster information and list the cluster nodes.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Docker Desktop
uninstall.description: This will quit Docker Desktop, move the application to the Trash, and remind you to review any leftover Docker settings. Run these commands in order: osascript -e 'quit app "Docker"', osascript -e 'tell application "Finder" to delete POSIX file "/Applications/Docker.app"', and echo "Review ~/.docker and remove it manually if you no longer need the Docker settings.". This quits Docker Desktop, moves it to the Trash, and reminds you to review the Docker configuration directory before deleting it.
commands: osascript -e 'tell application "Finder" to delete POSIX file "/Applications/Docker.app"'
echo "Review ~/.docker and remove it manually if you no longer need the Docker settings."

upgrade.title: Upgrade Docker Desktop
upgrade.description: Backup your Docker settings, perform the upgrade, and verify the installation. Run these commands in order: cp -r ~/.docker ~/.docker_backup, open https://store.docker.com/editions/community/docker-ce-desktop-mac, open ~/Downloads/Docker.dmg, sudo cp -R /Volumes/Docker/Docker.app /Applications/, open -a Docker, and kubectl get nodes. This backs up your settings, downloads and installs the latest Docker Desktop release, starts Docker Desktop, and verifies that Kubernetes is still running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kubernetes is not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Docker Desktop may not have started properly or Kubernetes is not enabled.

**Fix:**
Restart Docker Desktop and ensure Kubernetes is enabled in settings.

troubleshooting.title: kubectl: command not found
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** kubectl is not installed or not in the PATH.

**Fix:**
Ensure Docker Desktop is installed and running, which includes kubectl.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:6443: connect: connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kubernetes API server is not running.

**Fix:**
Check Docker Desktop settings to ensure Kubernetes is enabled and restart Docker.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kubernetes may be taking too long to start or is in a crash loop.

**Fix:**
Restart Docker Desktop and check the logs for errors.

versionNotes.changes: Improved Kubernetes integration and added support for Kubernetes v1.30.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'Kubernetes master is running at https://127.0.0.1:6443' and a list of nodes when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure your Mac meets the minimum system requirements for Docker Desktop.
